text: Tell us about yourself and why you are applying to this program.
Describe a time when you had to adapt to a significant change. How did you handle it?
What is the most meaningful accomplishment in your career so far?
If you could have dinner with any person, living or dead, who would it be and why?
Tell us about a time you failed. What did you learn from it?
How would your colleagues describe you in three words?
What is one thing you would change about your industry?
Describe a situation where you had to work with someone who was difficult to get along with.
What book has had the greatest impact on your professional development?
If you were given $1 million to start a business, what would you create?
Tell us about a time you took a risk. What was the outcome?
What is a current global issue that you feel strongly about, and what would you do to address it?
Describe a time when you had to persuade someone to see things your way.
What unique perspective will you bring to the classroom?
If you could master any skill overnight, what would it be and why?

material: Your team has been working on a project for three months. One week before the deadline, a key team member announces they are leaving the company and cannot complete their portion of the work.
text: How would you handle this situation to ensure the project is delivered on time?

material: You are the only person from your department at a company-wide strategy meeting. The CEO presents a new initiative that you believe will negatively impact your team's work and morale.
text: What would you do in this meeting, and how would you communicate with your team afterward?
Tell us about a time you received critical feedback. How did you respond?
What would you do on your first day as CEO of your current company?
Describe a situation where you had to make a decision with incomplete information.
What is something people often misunderstand about your field of work?
Tell us about a time you went above and beyond what was expected of you.

material: You notice that a classmate in your MBA program has been submitting work that appears to be heavily plagiarized from online sources. Other students have noticed too, but no one has said anything.
text: What would you do in this situation?
What are your short-term and long-term career goals, and how will this program help you achieve them?
Describe a situation where you had to lead a team through an ambiguous or uncertain situation.
Tell us about a time you had to make an ethical decision in a professional setting.
What is one thing you would change about your current organization, and how would you go about changing it?
Describe a time when you had to balance competing priorities. How did you decide what to focus on?
Tell us about a project you initiated on your own without being asked.
How do you handle disagreements with your manager or supervisor?
Describe a time when you received negative feedback from a client or customer. How did you respond?
What is a recent trend in your industry that excites or concerns you, and why?
Tell us about a time you had to communicate a complex idea to a non-expert audience.
Describe a situation where you had to work with people from different cultural backgrounds. What did you learn?
If you could solve one problem in your community, what would it be and how would you approach it?
Tell us about a time when your initial approach to a problem did not work. What did you do next?
What qualities do you think are most important in a leader, and how do you embody them?
Describe a time when you had to motivate others who were resistant or disengaged.
What would your direct reports or teammates say is your biggest area for improvement?
Tell us about a time you challenged the status quo at your workplace.
How do you stay informed about developments outside your own field or industry?
Describe a situation where you had to deliver results under a very tight deadline.
What is the most creative solution you have come up with to solve a business problem?
Tell us about a time when you volunteered for something outside your comfort zone.
If you had unlimited resources, what business would you start and why?
Describe a professional relationship that has been particularly important to your development.
What do you believe is the biggest challenge facing business leaders today?
Tell us about a time you had to say no to an opportunity. Why did you turn it down?
How would you contribute to a diverse and inclusive classroom environment?
Describe a situation where data or evidence changed your mind about something.
What is something you have taught yourself, and how did you go about learning it?
Tell us about a time when you had to manage a conflict between two other people.
What role does technology play in the future of your industry?
Describe a time when you took on a responsibility that was not part of your job description.
What is one misconception people often have about you, and why do you think it exists?
Tell us about a time when you had to quickly build trust with a new team or stakeholder.
How do you approach making decisions when the right answer is not clear?
What experience has most shaped your values or worldview?
Describe a time when you helped someone else succeed or grow professionally.
What is one thing you wish more people understood about your profession or background?
Tell us about a time you had to pivot your strategy mid-project. What triggered the change?
How do you measure success in your professional life?

material: You are leading a product launch, and one week before the launch date, your lead engineer informs you that a critical feature will not be ready in time. Delaying the launch would disappoint customers who have already been notified, but launching without the feature could damage the product's reputation.
text: What would you do, and how would you communicate your decision to stakeholders?

material: Your company has just acquired a smaller competitor. You have been asked to lead the integration of the two teams. Employees from the acquired company are anxious about job security, and your own team members are concerned about changes to their roles.
text: How would you approach the first 30 days of this integration?

material: You are a consultant advising a mid-size retailer. Their online sales have grown 40% year-over-year, but their physical store traffic has declined by 25%. The CEO wants to close half of their stores to invest more in e-commerce.
text: What factors would you consider before making a recommendation, and what would you advise?

material: A trusted colleague confides in you that they are planning to leave the company in two months. They are leading a high-profile project that is critical to the quarter's targets. Your manager is unaware of their plans.
text: How would you handle this situation?

material: You are managing a global team spread across three time zones. A new company policy requires all teams to have a daily 30-minute standup meeting. Finding a time that works for everyone would mean some team members must join very early in the morning or late at night.
text: How would you implement this policy while maintaining team morale and productivity?

material: You have been given a budget of $50,000 to improve employee engagement at your 200-person company. Recent surveys show that employees feel disconnected from leadership and that there are limited growth opportunities.
text: How would you allocate this budget, and what specific initiatives would you propose?

material: Your team has just lost a major client who represented 20% of your division's revenue. The client cited slow response times and lack of innovation as their reasons for leaving. Your CEO wants a plan to win the client back.
text: What steps would you take in the short term and long term to address this situation?

material: You are evaluating two candidates for a senior position on your team. Candidate A has 15 years of industry experience and a strong track record but has received feedback about being difficult to collaborate with. Candidate B has 5 years of experience, is highly collaborative, but lacks some of the technical skills required for the role.
text: Who would you hire and why? What would your onboarding plan look like?

material: A nonprofit organization you volunteer with has received a large donation from a corporation with a controversial environmental record. Some board members want to accept the donation because the organization desperately needs funding. Others want to decline it to protect the organization's reputation.
text: What would you recommend, and how would you facilitate a decision among the board?

material: You are a project manager at a tech startup. Your company just signed a contract promising delivery in 8 weeks, but after reviewing the scope with your engineering team, you realize the project realistically requires 12 weeks to complete properly.
text: How do you handle the gap between the commitment and reality? Walk us through your approach.

material: Your company is expanding into a new international market. Early market research suggests strong demand, but a local competitor dominates with 60% market share. Your budget for the first year is limited.
text: What market entry strategy would you recommend, and how would you differentiate from the local competitor?

material: You are part of a cross-functional task force examining whether your company should adopt artificial intelligence tools to automate parts of your customer service operations. The customer service team is concerned about job losses, while the finance team is focused on cost savings.
text: How would you approach this decision, and what would you recommend to senior leadership?
If you could witness any event in history firsthand, what would it be and why?
Describe a time when you had to represent an unpopular opinion or position. How did you handle it?
What is the most important lesson you have learned from a mentor?
Tell us about a time when you identified an opportunity that others had overlooked.
How would you explain what you do at work to a five-year-old?
Describe a situation where you had to make a trade-off between quality and speed.
What is one thing you are passionate about outside of your professional life, and how does it shape who you are?

material: You have just been promoted to manage a team of eight people, including two individuals who also applied for the same position. One of them has been openly critical of the decision in team settings.
text: How would you establish yourself as the team leader while addressing the tension?

material: Your company's flagship product has received a wave of negative reviews on social media after a recent update. The engineering team insists the update improved performance, but customers are unhappy with the new user interface. The PR team wants to issue a public statement immediately.
text: What course of action would you recommend, and how would you balance the needs of different stakeholders?
